paper.title: A VLA search for compact radio sources in the explosive molecular outflows DR 21 and G5.89

review.summary: Six internal tables: VLA observation log and image properties (tab:obs), compact radio sources in DR21 with combined-epoch fluxes (tab:crsM), multiwavelength counterparts of compact radio sources (tab:count), arc-shaped structure flux densities and parabola-fit parameters (tab:par_fit), compact radio sources in G5.89 (tab:crsGf), and appendix table with individual-epoch LSB/USB fluxes for DR21 sources (tab:crsA). External resources: VLA data-reduction pipeline, confitti GitHub/PyPI repositories and notebooks, and lmfit documentation.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarises the VLA observing configuration and image properties for DR21 and G5.89, including dates, sub-bands, synthesized beam shapes, and rms noise levels.

columns.name: Target
columns.meaning: Target source (DR21 or G5.89)

columns.name: Date | (07/2023)
columns.meaning: Observation date in July 2023

columns.name: Sub- | band
columns.meaning: Frequency sub-band (FB=full band, LSB=lower side band, USB=upper side band)

columns.name: Synthesized beam | (\theta_\rm maj.\times\theta_\rm min.; P.A.)
columns.meaning: Synthesized beam major axis, minor axis, and position angle

columns.name: rms noise | (\muJy bm^-1)
columns.meaning: Root-mean-square noise level measured near the image centre

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Catalogues 13 compact radio sources detected in DR21, reporting positions, integrated and peak flux densities, spectral indices, epoch-resolved fluxes, and variability fractions.

columns.meaning: Source identifier number

columns.meaning: Declination offset from 42d19'

columns.name: S_\nu,\rm int\supa | (\muJy)
columns.meaning: Integrated flux density in the combined-epoch image

columns.name: S_\nu,\rm peak\supa | (\muJy bm^-1)
columns.meaning: Peak flux density in the combined-epoch image

columns.name: \alpha
columns.meaning: Radio spectral index between LSB and USB

columns.meaning: Integrated flux density in epoch 1

columns.name: S_\nu,\rm peak,1 | (\muJy bm^-1)
columns.meaning: Peak flux density in epoch 1

columns.name: S_\nu,\rm int,2 | (\muJy)
columns.meaning: Integrated flux density in epoch 2

columns.name: S_\nu,\rm peak,2 | (\muJy bm^-1)
columns.meaning: Peak flux density in epoch 2

columns.name: Var. | (%)
columns.meaning: Percent flux variability between the two epochs

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists multiwavelength counterparts (infrared and X-ray) for compact radio sources in DR21, together with source classifications, references, and radio luminosities.

columns.meaning: Literature reference for the counterpart

internal_tables.evidence: Counterparts to compact radio sources of \dr.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports the flux densities and best-fit parabola parameters for nine arc-shaped radio structures in DR21, used to identify the ionising sources of the wind-driven shells.

source_refs.caption: Arc-shaped structure flux densities and parameters of the best parabola fit for each arc of \dr.
source_refs.label: tab:par_fit

columns.meaning: Arc identifier

columns.meaning: Integrated flux density of the arc

columns.name: S_\nu,\rm peak\supa | mJy beam^-1
columns.meaning: Peak flux density of the arc

columns.name: x_f\supb | 20^\rm h39^\rm m[^\rm s]
columns.meaning: Right ascension of the parabola focus

columns.meaning: Distance between the focus and the parabola apex

columns.name: \theta \supd | [^\circ]
columns.meaning: Orientation angle of the parabola measured from north to east

internal_tables.evidence: Arc-shaped structure flux densities and parameters of the best parabola fit for each arc of \dr.

external_resources.description: VLA automated data-reduction pipeline used for calibration and imaging of the radio interferometric observations.

source_refs.context:
Applications \citep[CASA, ][]{casa2022}. The pipeline includes flagging 
of flawed data and obtaining gain and phase calibration\footnote{NRAO 
gives a detailed description of the pipeline data editing and calibration at \url{https://science.nrao.edu/facilities/vla/data-processing/pipeline}.}.

For \dr\ after data calibration, a series of images were done in CASA using the task {\it tclean}. First, the FB of the two sessions were combined and imaged together, which allowed the most sensitive image to extended radio sources (see Fig.~\ref{fig:streams}). Second, the two combined sessions and each session were imaged using baselines above 300\,k$\lambda$, allowing to filter out radio sources with sizes larger than 0\rlap{.}$''$69. Each \dr session was imaged in Stokes V, and they were divided into two sub-bands; the lower-side band (LSB) and the upper-side band (USB) images were obtained to cover the frequency ranges from 12 to 15 GHz and from 15 to 18\,GHz, respectively. In all cases, \dr\ images were constructed using square pixels with a size of 0\rlap{.}$''$02, image size of 2700 pixels per side, and a uniform weighting scheme.

of flawed data and obtaining gain and phase calibration\footnote{NRAO 
gives a detailed description of the pipeline data editing and calibration at \url{https://science.nrao.edu/facilities/vla/data-processing/pipeline}.}.

For \dr\ after data calibration, a series of images were done in CASA using the task {\it tclean}. First, the FB of the two sessions were combined and imaged together, which allowed the most sensitive image to extended radio sources (see Fig.~\ref{fig:streams}). Second, the two combined sessions and each session were imaged using baselines above 300\,k$\lambda$, allowing to filter out radio sources with sizes larger than 0\rlap{.}$''$69. Each \dr session was imaged in Stokes V, and they were divided into two sub-bands; the lower-side band (LSB) and the upper-side band (USB) images were obtained to cover the frequency ranges from 12 to 15 GHz and from 15 to 18\,GHz, respectively. In all cases, \dr\ images were constructed using square pixels with a size of 0\rlap{.}$''$02, image size of 2700 pixels per side, and a uniform weighting scheme.

external_resources.description: GitHub repository for confitti, the Python package used to fit conic sections (parabolas) to the observed arc-shaped radio structures.

source_refs.context:
The process consists of two steps. 
First, the arc is traced with a series of points  
manually defined following the trace of the maximum intensity contours until reaching 3-$\sigma$ or finding other structures. Second, the selected points are fitted using a custom Python routine \footnote{Developed by W. Henney; the code is available at \url{https://github.com/div-B-equals-0/confitti}.} to derive a parabola.
This routine is described in Appendix \ref{app:pap} and employs a two-step approach: first, it performs a numerical minimization to derive the best-fit curve. Then, it carries out a Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) analysis to derive the uncertainties in the curve parameters. The best-fit parameters derived for each arc are presented in Table~\ref{tab:par_fit}, and a detailed view of the fit is shown in Figure~\ref{fig:arcs}.

external_resources.description: Python Package Index entry for confitti, used for conic-section fitting of radio arcs.
Documentation for the lmfit Python package used for non-linear least-squares minimisation in the analysis.
GitHub repository for confitti, the Python package used to fit conic sections (parabolas) to the observed arc-shaped radio structures.
